rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Sec 877.6
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 8, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTS ZACH GEORGOPOULOUS, GEORGOPOULOUS AND ECONOMIDIS' Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 877.6. The motion is granted. Defendants present substantial evidence showing that plaintiffs' probable recovery is in the vicinity of $100,000 and the $65,000 settlement is in good faith. The objecting party fails to establish that the settlement lacks good faith. See CCP 877.6(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EASTERN PLUMAS HEALTHCARE DISTRICT, A POLITICAL SU et al VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motionto Stay This Case Pending Resolution Of An Earlier-Filed Federal Case Raising Identical Claims And Memerandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 8, 2014, Line 3, RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES, TOBY DOUGLAS' Motion to Stay This Case Pending Resolution Of An Earlier-Filed Federal Case Raising Identical Claims. The motion for stay is granted. The federal and state complaints raise the same issue, i.e., whether AB 97 (which reduces the Medi-Cal payment rates to petitioners) violates various portions of 42 USC 1396a. The parties in the federal and state actions are the same/substantially similar. Petitioners concede that they are members of the federal plaintiff (CHA). There is clearly a chance for an unseemly conflict. The other factors highlighted in Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co., (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, also weigh in respondents' favor. The federal court is in the best position to apply federal law. The federal action has already been heavily litigated and is hopefully close to resolution. The stay will not prejudice petitioners. First, it is dubious whether petitioners can recover retroactive monetary relief in state court based on principles of sovereign immunity. See Alden v. Maine (1999) 527 U.S. 706, 712. Second, respondent represents that it does not plan to seek reimbursement for at least 6 months. (Gassman dec. par. 6). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SINGLER-ERNSTER, INC. VS. ROUND TABLE FRANCHISE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award And Memo Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 8, 2014, Line 4, RESPONDENT ROUND TABLE FRANCHISE CORPORATION'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees Incurred In Defeating Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Obtaining Confirmation Of Award. Granted. Respondent Round Table shall recover $22,000 in costs, including reasonable attorney's fees, from Petitioner Singler-Ernster. Round Table was the prevailing party in the post-arbitration matters, as the Court denied Singler-Ernster's motion to vacate the arbitration award. "A court must award costs in a judicial proceeding to confirm, correct or vacate an arbitration award" and in such proceedings, "[a]ttorney fees are recoverable as costs if authorized by contract." (Corona v. Amherst Partners (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 701, 707.) The Franchise Agreements provide attorney fees are recoverable in "any legal proceeding involving the enforcement of any of the provisions in this Agreement." (Lowenthal Declaration, Ex. 1, Franchise Agreement, Section 23.) In its Petition to Vacate the Arbitration Award, Singler-Ernster argued that the non-compete provisions were unenforceable and therefore the Arbitrator erred. The Court rejected this argument. Thus, Round Table had to incur fees in a "legal proceeding involving the enforcement of any of the provisions in this Agreement." (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD N WILSON VS. WESTSIDE COMMUNITY SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoena And For A Protective Order Against Deposition Fo Barbara Garcia
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 8, 2014, Line 2, OTHER BARBARA GARCIA Motion To Quash Subpoena And For A Protective Order Against Deposition of Barbara Garcia. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties are to appear and argue. Please be prepared to address which other individuals can provide the information requested from Mrs. Garcia regarding the composition of the Board. Both parties should bring proposed orders. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 8, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT NINA MACKENZIE'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Counsel Robert Devired Per CCP 128.7(B) & (C). Off calendar. Subsequent to the filing of this Demurrer, the Plaintiff filed a Motion for Consolidation of this case and probate case number PES-14-298241. A hearing for that Motion has been set for February 4, 2015 in Department 204. Pursuant to the Court's authority under California Rule of Court 3.515, the Court orders this action to be stayed pending the outcome of Plaintiff's Motion for Consolidation in case number PES-14-298241.
